ops.title: ship v0.17.0: scoped commits + trunk bookmark guard (foreign-bookmark incident resolved)
ops.body:
**What shipped (v0.17.0, tag on 0d332361c877; feature commit e0e07fb7e3ea):**

- `ccx vcs ship -m "<msg>" [paths...]` — trailing paths scope the commit. jj lane appends `-- <paths>` to `jj commit`/`jj squash` (filesets; the remainder stays in the new `@`); git lane swaps blanket `git add -A` for `git add -A -- <paths>` + pathspec-limited commit. File-granular by design, not hunk-granular.
- jj push guard: default only auto-advances the trunk bookmark (name from `jj log -r trunk() -T remote_bookmarks…`, deduped — colocated repos emit `main main` from main@git + main@origin). Foreign/multiple/unresolvable nearest bookmarks refuse; `--bookmark <name>` overrides. This closes the incident class where ship advanced and pushed a foreign bookmark `@` was parked on (yasyf/lag-probe-ch in captain-hook), sweeping concurrent-session commits onto the wrong remote branch.

**Two review-caught jj traps worth remembering:**
1. jj bookmark NAME args are **globs by default** (jj 0.43) — `jj bookmark move 'release-*'` moves every match. Ship anchors with `exact:` on both move and push.
2. `jj bookmark move <nonexistent>` warns and **exits 0** (silent no-op) — ship pre-resolves via the `bookmarks(exact:"<name>")` revset and refuses `bookmark not found`, otherwise it would have reported "pushed X → origin" with nothing published.

**Operational notes:**
- Landing dogfooded the feature: scoped ship of 8 files out of a shared `@` holding another session's uncommitted README/mcpserver work. It hit a transient `.git/index.lock` race with the concurrent session mid-`jj commit` (commit landed, bookmark move rolled back) — resume is `jj bookmark move exact:main --to @-` + `jj git push --bookmark exact:main` + watch CI; do NOT re-run ship (cuts a second, empty commit).
- Fleet guidance updated: cc-skills fragments ccx.md (entry 13) + version-control.md (partial-staging bypass line removed) in fd24afa7f940; cc-context's AGENTS.md "Version control" paragraph is a LOCAL fragment duplicate (.claude/fragments/AGENTS.md/go-style.fragment.md), edited separately — the shared version-control.md import serves other repos.
- Pre-existing, unguarded by design: ship on a clean `@` (or a zero-match fileset) still creates and pushes an empty commit.
